headline: When states override environmental law, the environment has no government — and 42 are dead
context: Malaysia's Constitution does not define 'environment.' DOE can object but states can override. Parliament passed forest protection reforms in 2022. Three years later, only Perlis and Federal Territories have adopted them.

cards.big: 42 died at sites where EIA rules were overridden — the mechanism is constitutional and most states refuse the reform that would fix it
cards.sub: DOE rejected Tanjung Bungah; 11 died. Batang Kali campsite was in a No-Go Area; 31 died. Parliament's 2022 NFA reforms have been adopted only by Perlis and FT.

cards.big: The word 'environment' is absent from the Federal Constitution — DOE can object but states can proceed regardless
cards.sub: NFA enacted under Article 76(1)(b) to promote uniformity but states must voluntarily adopt under Article 76(3). Planning authorities can and do override DOE rejections.

cards.big: Satellite forest-loss estimates far exceed official figures — and Johor's largest excision was carried out secretly
cards.sub: 15,011 ha excised in 2014; state forestry couldn't justify it to auditors. Kedah logs Ulu Muda despite federal ban — it supplies 80% of Penang's water.

cards.big: States resist NFA reforms because land conversion generates revenue that federal transfers don't replace
cards.sub: Pahang's bauxite boom: no EIA, RM46.7M royalties, 15km coastline contaminated. The pattern crosses party lines. The 42 dead were overwhelmingly low-income and migrant workers.

cards.big: This isn't development versus environment — it's a system where states profit from conversion and the people who die are never the people who approve
Like a building code that only applies in the lobby — each floor sets its own rules, and nobody checks the wiring
cards.sub: Federal environmental standards without state enforcement create a protection gap where disasters concentrate

cards.big: Either amend the Constitution to give DOE enforcement power, or make conservation more profitable than conversion through fiscal transfers
